symptom: a slowly enlarging neck with breathlessness and trouble swallowing when lying flat
hook: a big soft tissue mass was pushing down from the neck into the top of the chest and shoving the windpipe sideways
whatYouSee: A superior mediastinal mass extending up into the neck that pushes the trachea to one side and narrows it.
whyItMatters: An enlarged thyroid has grown down behind the breastbone where it can compress the trachea and the oesophagus and the great vessels.
treatment: Treatment is surgical removal when it compresses nearby structures or keeps growing.
takeaway: A neck mass diving behind the breastbone and shoving the trachea is a retrosternal goiter.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a slowly enlarging neck with breathlessness and trouble swallowing when lying flat.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a big soft tissue mass was pushing down from the neck into the top of the chest and shoving the windpipe sideways.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Retrosternal goiter

👀 What you see:
A superior mediastinal mass extending up into the neck that pushes the trachea to one side and narrows it.

🦴 Why it matters:
An enlarged thyroid has grown down behind the breastbone where it can compress the trachea and the oesophagus and the great vessels.

📝 Takeaway:
A neck mass diving behind the breastbone and shoving the trachea is a retrosternal goiter.
